---
title: "CV"
subparagraph: yes
geometry:
  - top=20mm
  - left=20mm
  - right=20mm
  - bottom=20mm
format:
    quarto-cv-pdf: 
        keep-tex: true
        pdf-engine: lualatex
author: Frederick J. Boehm
date: last-modified
phone: 715-252-3929
address: "University of Michigan · 419 Waymarket DR · Ann Arbor, Michigan 48103 · USA"
email: frederick.boehm@gmail.com
fontsize: 12pt
github: fboehm # github account
orcid: 0000-0002-1644-5931 # orcid identifier
#osf: ymqui # osf five character id
#twitter: twitter # twitter handle
web: fboehm.us
fontawesome: yes
nocite: '@*'
bibliography: 
  peer: parsing-bib-files/boehm-article.bib
  collection: parsing-bib-files/boehm-incollection.bib
  software: parsing-bib-files/boehm-software.bib
  submitted: parsing-bib-files/boehm-unpublished.bib
  book: parsing-bib-files/boehm-book.bib
  misc: parsing-bib-files/boehm-misc.bib
  proceedings: parsing-bib-files/boehm-inproceedings.bib
validate-yaml: false
csl: chicago-fullnote-bibliography-boehm.csl
engine: jupyter
---






# Education and Training

\hrule

I need to do something about the nbsp... buyt only if I want html otuput



**University of Michigan** \hfill\nbsp [Ann Arbor, Michigan, USA]{style="float:right"}    
Postdoctoral Research Fellow, Department of Internal Medicine (NIH T32 training grant) \hfill\ [2023 to present]{style="float:right"} 
Postdoctoral Research Fellow, Department of Biostatistics \hfill\ [2021 to 2023]{style="float:right"}

**University of Massachusetts Medical School** \hfill\ [Worcester, Massachusetts, USA]{style="float:right"}    
Postdoctoral Research Fellow, Department of Microbiology & Physiological Systems \hfill\ [2019 to 2021]{style="float:right"}   

**University of Wisconsin-Madison** \hfill\ [Madison, Wisconsin, USA]{style="float:right"}  
Postdoctoral Research Fellow, Department of Biostatistics and Medical Informatics \hfill\ [2019]{style="float:right"}  
Ph.D., Department of Statistics \hfill\ [2011 to 2019]{style="float:right"}  
Postdoctoral Research Fellow, Molecular and Environmental Toxicology Center \hfill\ [2012 to 2014]{style="float:right"}  
Assistant Researcher, Value-Added Research Center \hfill\ [2011]{style="float:right"}  


**University of Washington** \hfill\ [Seattle, Washington, USA]{style="float:right"}  
Postdoctoral Research Fellow, Department of Biostatistics \hfill\ [2007 to 2009]{style="float:right"}   

**University of Wisconsin-Madison** \hfill\ [Madison, Wisconsin, USA]{style="float:right"}  
M.D., School of Medicine and Public Health \hfill\ [2003 to 2007]{style="float:right"}   
M.S., Department of Population Health Sciences \hfill\ [2003 to 2007]{style="float:right"}   
B.S., with Honors in Chemistry, Mathematics, and Biochemistry \hfill\ [1996 to 2001]{style="float:right"}  



# Written Works 

\hrule

## Submitted Manuscripts 

::: {#refs-submitted}
:::

## Journal Articles 


### Journal articles, too

::: {#refs-peer}
:::

## Book  

::: {#refs-book}
:::

## Book Chapters

::: {#refs-collection}
:::

## Software

::: {#refs-software}
:::

## Conference Proceedings

::: {#refs-proceedings}
:::




## Other Publications

::: {#refs-misc}
:::


# Teaching Experience

\hrule

## Formal Courses

How to teach inclusively for educating a diverse nation  \hfill Spring 2017 & Fall 2017
Role: Co-facilitator \hfill University of Wisconsin-Madison & CIRTL Network  


Introductory statistics for biologists  \hfill Fall 2016 
Role: Instructor  \hfill University of Wisconsin-Madison

Special Topics: Data Science  \hfill Fall 2016   
Roles: Guest lecturer on topic models; Teaching assistant \hfill University of Wisconsin-Madison

Introductory statistics for engineers  \hfill Spring 2014, Fall 2014, Spring 2015, Summer 2015, Fall 2015, Spring 2016 
Role: Instructor \hfill University of Wisconsin-Madison

Introduction to Biostatistics  \hfill Fall 2009 & Winter 2010 
Role: Teaching assistant \hfill University of Washington

Introductory organic chemistry \hfill Fall 2001 
Role: Teaching assistant \hfill University of California, Berkeley




## Short Courses & Workshops

Engaging students in the teaching of statistics \hfill March 2018 & July 2020 
Role: Instructor  \hfill CIRTL Network


Data analysis with topic models for communications researchers \hfill July 2016 & October 2016
Role: Instructor  \hfill University of Wisconsin-Madison


Data Carpentry Workshop \hfill June 2017 & September 2018 (University of Wisconsin-Madison & Missouri S&T)  
Role: Instructor  \hfill University of Wisconsin-Madison & Missouri S&T University
Website: https://uw-madison-aci.github.io/2017-06-19-uwmadison-dc/ & https://yeemey.github.io/2018-09-15-und-mbdh/

Topic modeling for non-statisticians \hfill October 2013 
Role: Instructor \hfill University of Wisconsin-Madison

Statistical computing with R \hfill June 2012 & June 2013 
Role: Instructor \hfill University of Wisconsin-Madison


## Training in Teaching  





# Mentoring Experience

```{python} 
import yaml

# Read YAML file
with open("data/mentoring.yaml", "r") as file:
    data = yaml.safe_load(file)

# Select elements
first_person_name = data['people'][0]['name']
```

```{python} 
#| eval: false
from datetime import datetime

# Input date as a string
date_string = "2015-05-01"

# Parse the string into a datetime object
date_object = datetime.strptime(date_string, "%Y-%m-%d")

# Format the date as "Month Year"
formatted_date = date_object.strftime("%B %Y")

print(formatted_date)

```




Research mentor to `{python} data['people'][0]['name']`, `{python} data['people'][0]['title']` \hfill `{python} datetime.strptime(data['people][0]['start'], "%Y-%m-%d").strftime("%B %Y")` to `{python} data['people'][0]['end']`  
Project: `{python} data['people'][0]['project']`  
Next position: `{python} data['people'][0]['next_role']`  


Research mentor to Jinyu Xia, Undergraduate researcher  
Project: Tweet Sentiments Associate with Stock Market Trends  
Dates: September 2015 to August 2016  
Next Position: Graduate student at University of Washington (Department of Statistics)  


# Awards & Honors




# Talks and Presentations

# Service

## University Service

## Community Service

## Professional Service
